question_text: Which of the following is an example of a longitudinal wave?

options.label: A
options.text: A wave on the surface of water

options.label: B
options.text: A sound wave travelling through air

options.label: D
options.text: A wave along a vibrating rope

explanation: Sound waves are longitudinal because air particles vibrate back and forth in the same direction as the wave travels, creating compressions and rarefactions. Water waves, light, and rope waves are all transverse waves.
